description: Comprehensive eval covering all tiers (1-6), all command types, and behavioral checks

evals.prompt: Summarize the Wikipedia article on quantum computing. Give me 5 key points in bullet form.
evals.expected_output: 5 bullet points about quantum computing drawn from live Wikipedia content (qubits, superposition, entanglement, speedup, applications)

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract
assertions.description: Ran nimble extract targeting en.wikipedia.org

assertions.name: no_render_flag
assertions.description: Did NOT use --render (Wikipedia is static, Tier 1 should suffice)

assertions.name: five_bullets
assertions.description: Response contains at least 5 bullet points

assertions.name: covers_quantum_concepts
assertions.description: Mentions at least 2 of: qubit, superposition, entanglement, quantum gate, algorithm

assertions.name: attributes_nimble
assertions.description: Response credits Nimble or mentions the tool used as the data source

evals.prompt: What are the top 5 stories on Hacker News right now? Show title, score, and number of comments for each.
evals.expected_output: A list of 5 current HN stories with title, points, and comment count — live data

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract
assertions.description: Ran nimble extract targeting news.ycombinator.com

assertions.name: five_stories
assertions.description: Response lists at least 5 stories

assertions.name: includes_scores
assertions.description: Response includes numeric point/score values

assertions.name: attributes_nimble
assertions.description: Response credits Nimble as the source

evals.prompt: Find the top 3 Italian restaurants in San Francisco on Yelp. Show name, rating, and number of reviews.
evals.expected_output: 3 SF Italian restaurants with name, star rating, and review count from live Yelp data

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract_yelp
assertions.description: Ran nimble extract targeting yelp.com

assertions.name: uses_render_or_stealth
assertions.description: Command uses --render and/or --driver vx10-pro or similar (Yelp blocks without it)

assertions.name: three_restaurants
assertions.description: Response lists at least 3 restaurants

assertions.name: includes_ratings
assertions.description: Response includes star ratings

assertions.name: attributes_nimble
assertions.description: Response credits Nimble as the source

evals.prompt: Search Amazon for noise cancelling headphones under $200. Show me the top 5 results with price, rating, and product URL.
evals.expected_output: 5 Amazon headphone results with prices, ratings, and product URLs (e.g. amazon.com/dp/ASIN)

assertions.name: uses_amazon_search_url
assertions.description: Uses amazon.com/s? search URL, not a single /dp/ product page

assertions.name: five_results
assertions.description: Response lists at least 5 products

assertions.name: includes_prices
assertions.description: Response includes dollar prices

assertions.name: includes_product_urls
assertions.description: Response includes amazon.com/dp/ URLs for each product

assertions.name: attributes_nimble
assertions.description: Response credits Nimble as the source

evals.prompt: What are the current prices of Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana in USD? Use the CoinGecko API.
evals.expected_output: Live USD prices for BTC, ETH, and SOL from CoinGecko API (not from training data)

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract_xhr
assertions.description: Ran nimble extract with --is-xhr targeting api.coingecko.com

assertions.name: three_prices
assertions.description: Response contains prices for all 3 coins (BTC, ETH, SOL)

assertions.name: includes_dollar_amounts
assertions.description: Response includes dollar amounts (e.g. $85,000 or $85k)

assertions.name: no_render_flag
assertions.description: Did NOT use --render (CoinGecko is a JSON API, no rendering needed)

assertions.name: attributes_nimble
assertions.description: Response credits Nimble and mentions --is-xhr or API

evals.prompt: What's the latest news about OpenAI this week? Give me 5 headlines with a one-sentence summary each.
evals.expected_output: 5 recent OpenAI news headlines with summaries, sourced from live web search

assertions.name: uses_nimble_search
assertions.description: Ran nimble search with an OpenAI query

assertions.name: uses_news_focus
assertions.description: Used --focus news or --topic news or --time-range to get recent results

assertions.name: five_items
assertions.description: Response contains at least 5 items

assertions.name: mentions_openai
assertions.description: Response mentions OpenAI in the headlines

assertions.name: attributes_nimble
assertions.description: Response credits Nimble as the source and mentions the tool used

evals.prompt: What are the best libraries for building REST APIs in Python in 2026? Give me 5 recommendations with links.
evals.expected_output: 5 Python REST API library recommendations with URLs, sourced from live web search with coding focus

assertions.name: uses_nimble_search
assertions.description: Ran nimble search with a Python/REST API query

assertions.name: five_items
assertions.description: Response contains at least 5 recommendations

assertions.name: includes_python_libs
assertions.description: Mentions at least 2 of: FastAPI, Flask, Django, Litestar, Falcon, Bottle

assertions.name: attributes_nimble
assertions.description: Response credits Nimble as the source

evals.prompt: What are the main sections of the Python standard library documentation on docs.python.org? List the top-level categories.
evals.expected_output: A list of top-level Python stdlib doc sections discovered by mapping docs.python.org

assertions.name: uses_nimble_map
assertions.description: Ran nimble map targeting docs.python.org

assertions.name: lists_sections
assertions.description: Response lists multiple doc sections or URL paths

assertions.name: covers_stdlib_sections
assertions.description: Mentions at least 2 of: library, tutorial, reference, howto, extending, distributing

assertions.name: attributes_nimble
assertions.description: Response credits Nimble as the source and mentions map command

evals.prompt: What's on the GitHub Explore page right now? Show me the trending repositories.
evals.expected_output: Trending GitHub repos extracted live, followed by a feedback request to the user

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract
assertions.description: Ran nimble extract targeting github.com/explore or trending

assertions.name: lists_repos
assertions.description: Response lists at least 3 repository names

assertions.name: asks_for_feedback
assertions.description: Response ends with a question asking if results were useful, or presents feedback options

assertions.name: attributes_nimble
assertions.description: Response credits Nimble as the source

evals.tier: Behavioral — interactive menu on ambiguous prompt
evals.prompt: I want to scrape some data from a website.
evals.expected_output: Claude uses AskUserQuestion to present options (what site? what data? what format?) rather than guessing or asking in free-form prose

assertions.name: asks_clarifying_question
assertions.description: Response presents options or asks what site/data/format via menu or structured question

assertions.name: does_not_run_nimble_blindly
assertions.description: Does NOT immediately run nimble without knowing what site or data the user wants

assertions.name: offers_at_least_two_options
assertions.description: Presents at least 2 options or choices to the user
